<<<KOT:danaid>>>>
{"kind":"cannot-formalise","why":"danaid has two distinct senses: (1) a zoological taxon (butterfly family/subfamily), requiring technical Linnaean taxonomy grounding the catalog cannot provide; (2) a mythological figure from Greek legend, a proper-noun plural referring to specific named individuals in a canonical narrative. Neither sense is expressible in the prime lexicon or grounding-note register. The everyday sense, if any, is subordinate to these technical and proper-noun uses."}
<<<END:danaid>>>>

<<<KOT:deixis>>>>
{"kind":"cannot-formalise","why":"deixis is a technical concept from linguistics and semiotics. It requires the notion of 'linguistic reference constrained by utterance context' and 'indexicality', neither of which can be expressed in the prime lexicon. The definition turns on abstract properties of language (pronouns, particles, context-dependence, anaphora) that lie outside the scope of concrete things, bodily actions, and simple relations available in the controlled lexicon."}
<<<END:deixis>>>>
